commit.message:
fix(query): protect writer match phase with READ lock against defrag race (#1834)

* fix(query): protect writer match phase with READ lock against defrag race

Writer queries executed the match phase (scanning entities, reading
properties) without holding any graph lock. This allowed concurrent
memory modifications by active defrag or GRAPH.EFFECT on the main
thread to invalidate entity pointers mid-execution, causing heap
corruption and SIGSEGV crashes.

The fix acquires a graph READ lock for writer queries before execution
begins, protecting the match phase. At commit time (LockForCommit),
the READ lock is released before acquiring the GIL and WRITE lock,
maintaining the lock ordering invariant (GIL before graph lock) to
prevent deadlocks with main-thread write paths.

Lock flow for writers:
1. MarkWriter under GIL (no graph lock - avoids READ→GIL deadlock)
2. Acquire READ lock (protects match phase from defrag/EFFECT)
3. Execute query (match phase protected by READ lock)
4. LockForCommit: release READ → acquire GIL → acquire WRITE
5. Commit changes under WRITE lock
6. UnlockCommit: release WRITE + GIL

If commit never happens (e.g. read-only MERGE), the READ lock is
released after execution completes, including ResultSet_Reply.

Closes #1831

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(defrag): add concurrent writers regression test

Adds test_concurrent_writers_during_defrag to exercise the race
condition fixed in this PR: concurrent MERGE+SET writer queries
running while active defrag is enabled with aggressive thresholds.

The test verifies the server stays alive and data remains consistent,
catching the heap corruption crash that occurred before the fix.

Addresses review comment: add flow test for concurrent defrag+writes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): remove unsupported message arg from assertEquals

RLTest assertEquals does not support a third message argument
like Python unittest. This caused a TypeError in CI.

commit.message:
Remove periodic PEERS broadcaster that caused duplicate PEERS

The peer advertiser task periodically sent PEERS to all inbound peers,
but handle_accepted_inbound_peer already sends PEERS during connection
setup. The duplicate PEERS message triggered a protocol violation on
the remote side, dropping the connection.

stellar-core only sends PEERS once during recvAuth() (Peer.cpp:1947)
and has no periodic PEERS broadcaster.

Closes #1164

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix preferred peer inbound admission with eviction

Implement preferred-peer eviction for inbound connections, matching
stellar-core's acceptAuthenticatedPeer (OverlayManagerImpl.cpp:208-270).

When all inbound slots are full and a preferred peer connects:
1. Find a non-preferred authenticated inbound peer
2. Send ERR_LOAD to evict it
3. Force-promote the preferred peer

Add preferred_peers field to SharedPeerState so the connection handler
can check whether an incoming peer matches the configured preferred list.

Closes #1149

fix(smAgent): include target agent customParams in encoded_config

When SM triggers agents via old-style paths (agents/pr_review.json),
buildEncodedConfig was only forwarding configPath in customParams.
If dmtools replaces customParams entirely (vs merging), autoStartRework,
autoStartReworkConfigFile, targetRepository, etc. were lost — causing
the SM to re-trigger rework separately with the wrong (generic) path.

Fix: resolve project-specific agent JSON (ai_teammate/<projectKey>/pr_review.json)
and include its customParams as the base in encoded_config. configPath from
effectiveConfig still overrides to ensure the project config is always found.

Phase 5c: migrate setter lookups in property assignment/read to helpers

- VmFunction/VmClosure setter lookup: make_setter_key + .get() → lookup_setter (2 sites)
- VmObject setter-only check (read): make_setter_key + contains_key → has_setter (2 sites)
- VmArray setter-only check (read): make_setter_key + contains_key → has_setter
- VmFunction key_exists check: contains_key(&setter_key) → has_setter
- Eliminated 6 make_setter_key calls from vm.rs

commit.message:
fix: crypto-side playtest — BUG-041–048 (NFT minting, loot tiers, CAPS economy, scrap lock) (#217)

* Initial plan

* fix: crypto-side playtest bugs BUG-041 through BUG-048

BUG-041 (High) - mint-item.js: Replace non-atomic GET+SET daily-limit
check with atomic redis.incr() + expire() + decr-on-exceed to eliminate
TOCTOU race where concurrent requests could bypass the mint daily cap.

BUG-042 (High) - Tiered loot CAPS economy non-functional:
- loot-voucher.js: Calculate CAPS reward from POI rarity (RARITY_CAPS
  map with env-configurable values per tier) and sign it into the voucher
  as voucher.caps so the redemption amount is server-authenticated.
- gps.js: Include voucher.caps in serializeVoucherMessage (backward-
  compatible: old vouchers without caps field still verify correctly).
- redeem-voucher.js: Use voucher.caps directly (covered by NaCl
  signature) instead of broken LOOT_ID_TO_CAPS comma-delimited parser.

BUG-043 (Medium) - loot-voucher.js: Expand randomInt lootId range from
1,000,000 to 2^48 to dramatically reduce birthday-paradox PDA seed
collision probability for on-chain ClaimLoot.

BUG-044 (Medium) - redeem-voucher.js: Pre-call redis.key() in
VOUCHER_USED_KEY so voucher replay-protection keys are stored at
afw:afw:voucher:used:* (double-prefix, consistent with all other
app keys) instead of afw:voucher:used:* (orphaned single-prefix).

BUG-046 (Low) - caps.js: Add capsBalanceLimiter (30 req/min) to
GET /:wallet to prevent bulk wallet enumeration without authentication.

BUG-047 (Low) - player-state.js + dungeon.js: Add MAX_CAPS = 999_999_999
ceiling to awardCaps() and dungeon loot assignment to match backend limit
and prevent Pip-Boy display overflow.

BUG-048 (Low) - scrap-nft.js: Use per-NFT lock key (wallet+nftMint)
instead of per-wallet lock so concurrent scraps of different NFTs are
no longer unnecessarily serialized with 15-second waits.

Add 11 regression tests (BUG-041..048) — total test count: 94 → 105.
